hook.text: A prototype is a fake version of your product that feels real enough to test with users.

buildup.text: Prototypes range from paper sketches to clickable mockups to 'Wizard of Oz' setups with humans behind the curtain.

discovery.text: The goal isn't to build something pretty. It's to learn something fast — with the minimum investment of time.

twist.text: Teams often over-invest in prototype fidelity. A hand-drawn screen tested in 30 minutes beats a polished mockup next month.

climax.text: IDEO's motto: 'If a picture is worth a thousand words, a prototype is worth a thousand meetings.'

punchline.text: Don't debate the idea. Prototype it and test it.

quiz.question: What is the main goal of prototyping in product discovery?
options: Learning fast with minimum investment, not building something polished
Creating a fully functional product early
